Mrs. A. j. Schiller Is an anti-suf.
fragette. Il do not believe in the
cause of woman's suffrage" said
Mrs. Schiller. and | have no de-
sire to visit the polls. think it is
bad enough to have men constantly
before the public, let alone women


II do not believe that women are
incapable of Judging or even act
ing In the capacity Of officials, but
on the contrary. | believe they are
often more proficient than men, but
| do feel that affairs of public are
out of woman's sphere"


The views of Mrs. Sarah e
Commerford are expressed in the
following article:


I'm all advancement throughout
the world for the uplifting of hu.
mainly none has been so criticised
and questioned as to its results as
the enfranchisement of women.


when looked at from all points
of view it is only her natural de-
velopment. She has learned her
A B C's and now sees, through edu,
cation and experience, the neces'
sity of carrying her housekeeping
and mothering instinct out into the
busy world of things; hence her
right to be enfranchised is but just
and the voice of history"
